# Alembic Migrations — Command Reference

## Environment

| What | Value |
|---|---|
| Config file | `alembic.ini` (workspace root) |
| Migration env | `alembic/env.py` |
| Versions dir | `alembic/versions/` |
| Python runner | `uv run` |
| DB driver | `asyncpg` (Postgres only) |

---

## Full Command Reference

```bash
# State
uv run alembic current                    # show current revision(s)
uv run alembic history                    # show migration history
uv run alembic history --verbose          # with full details
uv run alembic heads                      # show head revision(s)
uv run alembic show <rev>                 # show a specific revision

# Upgrading
uv run alembic upgrade head               # apply all pending migrations
uv run alembic upgrade +1                 # apply one migration
uv run alembic upgrade <revision_id>      # upgrade to a specific revision

# Downgrading
uv run alembic downgrade -1               # roll back one migration
uv run alembic downgrade <revision_id>    # downgrade to a specific revision
uv run alembic downgrade base             # roll back everything

# Creating
uv run alembic revision -m "description"                     # blank migration
uv run alembic revision --autogenerate -m "description"      # auto-detect changes

# Stamping (mark state without running migrations)
uv run alembic stamp head                 # mark db as at head without migrating
uv run alembic stamp <revision_id>        # mark db at a specific revision
uv run alembic stamp base                 # clear alembic_version table

# Merging diverged heads
uv run alembic merge heads -m "merge_description"
uv run alembic merge <rev1> <rev2> -m "merge_description"
```

---

## SQLAlchemy Column Types Most Commonly Used Here

```python
import sqlalchemy as sa
from pgvector.sqlalchemy import Vector

sa.Integer()                    # int4
sa.BigInteger()                 # int8 (Discord IDs)
sa.String(N)                    # varchar(N)
sa.Text()                       # text (unbounded)
sa.Boolean()                    # bool
sa.DateTime(timezone=True)      # timestamptz
sa.DateTime(timezone=False)     # timestamp
Vector(384)                     # pgvector vector(384)

# Server defaults — always use SQL-compatible forms for Postgres:
server_default=sa.true()         # DEFAULT TRUE
server_default=sa.false()        # DEFAULT FALSE
server_default=sa.text("now()")  # DEFAULT now()
```

---

## op Functions Reference

```python
from alembic import op
import sqlalchemy as sa

# Tables
op.create_table("name", *columns, *constraints)
op.drop_table("name")
op.rename_table("old", "new")

# Columns
op.add_column("table", sa.Column("name", sa.Text(), nullable=True))
op.drop_column("table", "column")
op.alter_column("table", "column", nullable=False, server_default="x", new_column_name="y")

# Indexes
op.create_index("ix_name", "table", ["col1", "col2"])
op.drop_index("ix_name", "table")
op.create_unique_constraint("uq_name", "table", ["col"])
op.drop_constraint("uq_name", "table")

# Foreign keys
op.create_foreign_key("fk_name", "source_table", "ref_table", ["col"], ["ref_col"])
op.drop_constraint("fk_name", "source_table", type_="foreignkey")

# Raw SQL (needed for extensions, custom indexes, etc.)
op.execute(sa.text("CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector"))
op.execute(sa.text("CREATE INDEX ... USING ivfflat ..."))
```

---

## Autogenerate Limitations

Autogenerate will **not** detect:
- `CREATE/DROP EXTENSION` statements
- `Vector` columns (pgvector type is unknown to the comparator by default)
- IVFFlat / HNSW indexes
- Partial indexes
- Changes to `server_default` values on existing columns

Always review autogenerated files and add these manually.

---

## IVFFlat Index Tuning

`lists = 100` is a safe default for up to ~1M rows. General guideline:
- < 100K rows → `lists = 100` (or even lower)
- 100K–1M rows → `lists = sqrt(n_rows)` rounded up
- > 1M rows → consider HNSW instead: `USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 64)`

Cosine distance operator: `<=>` (use for normalized embeddings like MiniLM)
L2 distance operator: `<->` (use for raw embeddings)
